description: Enhanced presentation development workflow combining course lessons with professional best practices

source.enhanced: Professional presentation development best practices analysis

phases.name: Discovery & Research
phases.description: Understand your topic, audience, and context before planning

tasks.title: Research your topic deeply
tasks.description: Build subject matter expertise before you can teach others

tasks.title: Analyze competing or similar presentations
tasks.description: Review what has worked before in this space - TED talks, industry presentations, competitor decks

tasks.title: Identify constraints and logistics
tasks.description: Time limits, venue, technical setup, audience size, remote vs in-person

tasks.title: Define success metrics
tasks.description: How will you know if the presentation succeeded? What actions or outcomes do you want?

phases.name: The Start
phases.description: Define your audience and presentation goals

tasks.title: Stay out of PowerPoint!
tasks.description: Resist the urge to open PowerPoint too early in the process

tasks.title: Select a presentation for your project
tasks.description: Choose a real presentation to work on throughout the process

tasks.title: Identify the audience for your presentation
tasks.description: Determine who will be receiving your presentation

tasks.title: Create an audience map
tasks.description: Document key characteristics, needs, expectations, and knowledge level of your audience

tasks.title: Identify audience pain points and motivations
tasks.description: What problems does your audience face? What do they care about most?

tasks.title: Build the context portion of your introduction
tasks.description: Identify what your audience already knows and establish common ground

tasks.title: Determine the catalyst for the presentation
tasks.description: What has happened that has created the need for this presentation?

tasks.title: Determine the central question you are answering
tasks.description: Clarify the central question your presentation addresses

tasks.title: Write down a single goal or next step
tasks.description: Define the ultimate objective of your presentation - the ONE thing

tasks.title: Plan the Action Agenda for the first 72 hours
tasks.description: Think about the component pieces of your goal and immediate next steps

phases.name: Structure
phases.description: Generate and organize your ideas into a logical flow

tasks.title: Gather content to answer your question
tasks.description: Collect existing content, data, research, and new analysis

tasks.title: Run a brainstorming session
tasks.description: Generate new ideas and approaches - write everything down without judgment

tasks.title: Select a visual thinking tool
tasks.description: Choose a method to organize and visualize your thoughts (mind map, sticky notes, etc.)

tasks.title: Define your key message hierarchy
tasks.description: What's the ONE thing they must remember? What are the 3-5 supporting points?

tasks.title: Create an Argument Map
tasks.description: Visually map out your reasoning structure for your answer

tasks.title: Build an inductive argument
tasks.description: Build logical support for your conclusion from evidence to claim

tasks.title: Structure evidence into MECE groups
tasks.description: Organize supporting evidence into mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive groups (4-5 max)

tasks.title: Develop the 'so what?' for each point
tasks.description: For every piece of content, answer: Why should the audience care?

tasks.title: Build transitions between sections
tasks.description: Plan how ideas flow together - create bridges between major sections

tasks.title: Identify potential objections and questions
tasks.description: Anticipate pushback and prepare responses - address objections proactively

phases.name: Storytelling
phases.description: Craft your narrative arc and create the storyboard

tasks.title: Identify where to inject stories
tasks.description: Find opportunities for narrative elements in your presentation

tasks.title: Determine story placement
tasks.description: Consider adding stories at the beginning (hook), middle (illustration), or end (inspiration)

tasks.title: Design stories that stick
tasks.description: Make your stories memorable using concrete details, emotion, and unexpectedness

tasks.title: Gather supporting evidence for your stories
tasks.description: Stories need credibility - collect data, quotes, or visuals that reinforce them

tasks.title: Map the emotional arc
tasks.description: Plan where the highs and lows are - tension, relief, inspiration, call to action

tasks.title: Create your storyboard on paper
tasks.description: Draft your presentation flow on paper or sticky notes first - one idea per 'slide'

tasks.title: Iterate the storyboard several times
tasks.description: Refine focusing first on key messages, then flow, then supporting arguments

tasks.title: Test story resonance with a sample audience
tasks.description: Share your storyboard with 1-2 trusted people - does the narrative land?

phases.name: Design
phases.description: Apply visual design principles and create slides

lessons.title: Overview of the Fundamental Elements of Design
The Fundamental Elements of Design in Detail

tasks.title: Refine storyboard for visual balance
tasks.description: Strike the balance between text and visual forms (charts, graphs, diagrams)

tasks.title: Create a color palette
tasks.description: Use Adobe Color Wheel or similar tool to define your presentation's color scheme

tasks.title: Select custom fonts
tasks.description: Choose complementary fonts for headings and body text, download and install

tasks.title: Create a master slide template
tasks.description: Build consistent layouts before creating individual slides - title, content, section dividers

tasks.title: Design title slide and section dividers
tasks.description: First impressions matter - create a compelling opening visual

tasks.title: Build an icon and image library
tasks.description: Gather consistent visual assets - icons, photos, illustrations in a unified style

tasks.title: Review storyboard for design principle violations
tasks.description: Check whitespace, alignment, proximity, contrast, and repetition

tasks.title: Design your slides in detail
tasks.description: Move beyond storyboarding and start actual slide creation

tasks.title: Create a handout or leave-behind version
tasks.description: Design a version suitable for distribution after the presentation

phases.name: Data Visualization
phases.description: Create effective charts, graphs, and data displays

tasks.title: Validate data accuracy
tasks.description: Fact-check all data before visualizing - verify sources and calculations

tasks.title: Decide tables vs graphs for your data
tasks.description: Choose the right format for each data point based on the story you're telling

tasks.title: Create basic charts
tasks.description: Apply best practices for bar charts, line charts, and pie charts

tasks.title: Consider specialized chart types
tasks.description: Explore waterfall charts, scatter plots, and other advanced visualizations if appropriate

tasks.title: Add source citations to data
tasks.description: Include sources for credibility - small text at bottom of data slides

tasks.title: Create animation and build sequences
tasks.description: Plan progressive disclosure of complex data - reveal data points sequentially

tasks.title: Conduct peer review of content
tasks.description: Get expert feedback on accuracy, logic, and persuasiveness

tasks.title: Conduct design review
tasks.description: Fresh eyes on visual consistency, readability, and professional appearance

tasks.title: Perform accessibility check
tasks.description: Verify color contrast, font sizes, alt text for images, and screen reader compatibility

tasks.title: Complete legal and compliance review
tasks.description: If presenting externally, verify trademarks, confidential info, and regulatory compliance

tasks.title: Final proofread
tasks.description: Check for typos, grammar, consistency in terminology, and formatting

tasks.title: Incorporate feedback and finalize
tasks.description: Address review comments and lock the final version

phases.name: Performance
phases.description: Prepare, practice, and deliver your presentation

tasks.title: Write your voiceover script
tasks.description: Document what you will say for each slide - word for word initially

tasks.title: Create speaker notes
tasks.description: Condense script into bullet-point reminders for each slide - not full sentences

tasks.title: Memorize your script
tasks.description: Know your content well enough to deliver naturally without reading

tasks.title: Prepare for Q&A
tasks.description: Anticipate 10-15 likely questions and prepare concise answers

tasks.title: Create backup slides for Q&A
tasks.description: Build an appendix with supporting data for anticipated questions

tasks.title: Test presentation length and edit to fit time
tasks.description: Time yourself and cut or expand to fit the allotted time (aim for 80% to allow for Q&A)

tasks.title: Conduct technical rehearsal
tasks.description: Test equipment, clicker, screen resolution, audio/video playback in actual venue if possible

tasks.title: Practice in front of others and solicit feedback
tasks.description: Do at least 2-3 full run-throughs with a live audience

tasks.title: Record and review yourself presenting
tasks.description: Record video and watch it critically - note verbal tics, pacing, eye contact

tasks.title: Develop a 'Plan B' deck
tasks.description: Prepare a condensed 50% version for time-constrained situations

tasks.title: Prepare backup plans for technical failures
tasks.description: What if projector fails? No internet? Have PDF backup, printed handouts ready

tasks.title: Send follow-up materials
tasks.description: Distribute slides, handouts, or additional resources to attendees

tasks.title: Execute the Action Agenda
tasks.description: Follow through on the next steps you promised in the presentation

tasks.title: Collect feedback from attendees
tasks.description: Send a brief survey or request informal feedback on what worked

tasks.title: Conduct personal retrospective
tasks.description: What went well? What would you do differently? Document lessons learned

tasks.title: Archive presentation materials
tasks.description: Store final version, speaker notes, and feedback for future reference

kanbanMapping.description: Suggested mapping of tasks to kanban columns

backlog.name: Backlog
backlog.description: Tasks not yet ready to start

do.name: To Do
do.description: Tasks ready to be worked on

priorities.high: Critical path tasks that block other work

suggestedWorkflow.description: Tasks should generally flow through phases in order, but some parallel work is possible
